description_long: This object is a vintage cash register with a robust, rectangular wooden base and a metallic upper body finished in a light beige color. The front features a series of round, white keys arranged in vertical columns, each with dark markings, and a prominent lever on the right side for manual operation. Above the keys is a display window, likely for showing transaction amounts, framed by a raised panel. The sides are adorned with subtle decorative patterns and the right side includes a large, metallic lever. The back is plain and unadorned, emphasizing the utilitarian design. The overall construction suggests durability, with a combination of metal and wood materials typical of early 20th-century cash registers.
description: A vintage cash register with a wooden base, beige metallic body, rows of white keys, and a display window above the keypad. It features a prominent side lever and decorative side panels.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full keypad, display window, and the wooden base.
description_view_1: Right side view highlighting the large metallic lever and decorative paneling.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain, flat metallic back and the wooden base.
description_view_3: Left side view showing the curved metallic housing and the side of the wooden base.
attribute_keypad_layout: vertical columns of round keys, approximately 30 keys, labeled with numbers and symbols
